# MC Painting Maker

> Free, privacy-first, browser-based tool that turns any image into a custom Minecraft Bedrock painting and exports a ready-to-install `.mcaddon` add-on. Compatible with Bedrock Edition 1.21.40+ and Minecraft Education Edition. No upload, no account, no tracking: everything runs locally in the browser.

The site is a single-page web application. All work happens client-side: images, transforms, namespace, pack metadata, and the generated `.mcaddon` ZIP never leave the user's browser. Output is a standard Bedrock behavior pack + resource pack bundle that registers each painting as an independent custom entity, placeable through its own custom item (using the `minecraft:entity_placer` component) in a configurable Creative category.

## Core features

- Drop one or more images (PNG, JPG, WEBP) and choose a target size in Minecraft blocks (width x height).
- Crop, resize, and reposition the source image inside the painting canvas with a Konva-based editor.
- Per-painting in-game ID (slug) with namespace prefix; auto-derived from the name, lockable, and user-editable.
- Configurable pack metadata: pack name, namespace, version, icon, Creative inventory group.
- One-click export of a single `.mcaddon` containing both the behavior pack and the resource pack.
- Deterministic UUIDs that survive renames so re-imports update existing entities instead of duplicating them.
- Project state persisted in `localStorage`; nothing is uploaded.

## How a Bedrock player uses the output

1. Open the exported `.mcaddon` with Minecraft Bedrock; both packs install.
2. Create a world with the Behavior pack and the Resource pack enabled.
3. In the Creative inventory, open the `items` category and find the "Custom Paintings" group (name configurable).
4. Use a painting item against a wall; the painting appears flush, snaps to the nearest cardinal direction, and is hittable across its full surface. Punching it despawns it.

## Useful links

- [Application](https://mc-paintings.larosa.work/): the painting maker itself.
- [How to use](https://mc-paintings.larosa.work/how-to.html): step-by-step guide for creating paintings, installing the .mcaddon, and placing paintings in-game from the Creative inventory or via /give and /summon.
- [Privacy policy](https://mc-paintings.larosa.work/privacy.html): what data is and is not collected, in plain words.
- [Source code](https://github.com/Fabrimat/MC-Painting-Maker): MIT-licensed Svelte 5 + TypeScript + Vite project.
- [Issue tracker](https://github.com/Fabrimat/MC-Painting-Maker/issues): bugs, feature requests, and questions.
- [Support on Ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/fabriziolarosa): tip jar for the author.

## Tech stack

Svelte 5, TypeScript, Vite, Konva for the canvas editor, fflate for in-browser ZIP creation, Zod for schema validation, Vitest for tests. Deployed as a static site to GitHub Pages behind Cloudflare.

## Author

Fabrizio La Rosa (Belgian VAT BE1031214819, Ghent, Belgium). Contact via the GitHub repository.
